mainEntity.name: What are the four pillars of LANDSCAPE Photography?

acceptedAnswer.text: Location, light, composition, and processing. Scout scenes, plan the best light, compose with intent, then edit subtly to retain a natural look.

mainEntity.name: What is the golden rule in LANDSCAPE Photography?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the rule of thirds to place horizon and subject. Break it intentionally for symmetry or minimalism when the scene demands it.

mainEntity.name: How do I take high-quality landscape photos?

acceptedAnswer.text: Shoot RAW at base ISO, use a tripod, work golden/blue hour, include a clear focal point and foreground, and use filters (ND, polariser) when needed.

mainEntity.name: What settings are best for LANDSCAPE Photography?

acceptedAnswer.text: Start at ISO 100, f/8–f/11 for sharpness, and adjust shutter for exposure. Bracket in high contrast and focus stack for deep foreground detail.

mainEntity.name: When is the best time to shoot landscapes?

acceptedAnswer.text: Golden hour adds warmth and depth; blue hour balances sky and city lights. Midday can work for graphic shadows and black-and-white.

mainEntity.name: Which filters help in LANDSCAPE Photography?

acceptedAnswer.text: A polariser cuts glare and deepens skies. ND filters enable long exposures for water or clouds. Use with a tripod and stable exposure.

mainEntity.name: How do I keep horizons and lines straight?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use a tripod and in-camera level. Compose carefully, then fine-tune in post with transform tools to correct any remaining tilt.
